core_model.structure_summary: Strategic units on the nodes of a hypercube graph each maximize a payoff that is linear in a simplex-constrained allocation vector minus a linear cost activated by binary indicator variables tied to positive allocations, with best responses characterized by Karush-Kuhn-Tucker stationarity conditions and an added congestion cost producing an inefficient equilibrium.
core_model.canonical_form: \max_{x, v} \; \Pi_i = \sum_j (b_{ij} - c_{ij})\, v_{ij}\, x_{ij} \quad \text{s.t.} \quad \sum_j x_{ij} = 1,\; x_{ij} \ge 0,\; v_{ij} = \mathbf{1}[x_{ij} > 0];\qquad \mathcal{L} = \sum_j (b_{ij}-c_{ij}) x_{ij} + \lambda^{\top} h + \mu\Big(1 - \sum_j x_{ij}\Big)

variables.meaning: simplex-constrained allocation vector distributing a unit of mass across edges/destinations
binary indicators switching on exactly where the allocation is positive
per-destination cost coefficients, dominated by benefits on cross links
additional cost term increasing with aggregate use of a link (congestion externality)

deterministic_skeleton.summary: Maximization of a linear objective over the probability simplex with sign-activated cost terms, solved by Lagrangian stationarity with complementary-slackness conditions selecting which coordinates are positive.

notes: The equations are idiosyncratic and partly redundant (case tables for indicator variables, repeated constraint blocks). The core object is clearly the per-unit constrained linear maximization embedded in a multi-unit strategic setting on hypercube graphs; the 'paradox' result concerns the equilibrium when a congestion cost is appended. The congestion cost's exact functional form is not shown in the extracted equations, so its structure is inferred from the abstract.
